text.title: OpenAI Fine-Tuned Model Production Deployment — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `fine-tuning-openai-deployment` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Never deploy any fine-tuned model that has not passed the eval-gate on a held-out set.
rule.rationale: Deploying un-gated fine-tunes is the #1 cause of silent production regressions.

rule.statement: Ramp 10% → 50% → 100% with ≥24h at each step (≥1000 requests minimum).
rule.rationale: Statistical noise at <1000 samples masks regressions; full deploy hides what 10% would reveal.

rule.statement: Monitor inference cost vs base model; alert if cost ratio >2x for >1h.
rule.rationale: Fine-tuned models cost 1.5-2x base; runaway cost suggests longer outputs or retry storms.

rule.statement: Define rollback signal BEFORE ramp: latency p95 >2x baseline OR error_rate >1% OR quality_score_drop >5pts.
rule.rationale: Without pre-defined signals, rollback debates eat the regression window.

rule.statement: Store model_id in a version-controlled registry (yaml / json), never inline in code.
rule.rationale: Inline model IDs become un-rollbackable when code ships without the previous ID.

rule.statement: Polling of long-running fine-tune jobs uses async + exponential backoff (5s, 10s, 30s, 60s capped).
rule.rationale: Synchronous polling burns API quota and hits rate limits.
